title: rubus × sericatiformis
intro: Rubus × sericatiformis, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan black raspberry, is a hybrid species of flowering plant in the rose family, specifically a cross between Rubus armeniacus and Rubus fruticosus. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Rubus × sericatiformis include its potential to aid in cardiovascular health, improve digestion, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional medicine to make jams, jellies, and other food products.
